Angelica foeniculum is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its tall, hollow stems and umbrella-like clusters of small white flowers. The root and rhizome of Angelica foeniculum have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antispasmodic. The bioactive constituents of this plant include volatile oils, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Angelica foeniculum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried root and rhizome for use in cooking and as a spice.
